'''Howard Eugene Day''' ([[1950s#1951|1951]] – [[September 23]], [[1982]])<ref name="Lambiek" /> was a comic book inker and penciller and worked on several of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic books. He began his comics career in the mid 1970s creating underground comics in Canada, including a collaboration with celebrated independent creator [[Wikipedia:Dave Sim|Dave Sim]]. In the late 1970s he was inking regularly for [[Marvel Comics]] including their ''[[Star Wars]]'' series where he alternated inking duties with [[Bob Wiacek]]. He left the series soon after the adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], but returned to pencil two issues in the early 1980s and helped create the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] characters [[Fenn Shysa]] and [[Tobbi Dala]]. However, within months of those last two issues seeing print, Day died of a heart attack in his sleep.
